food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken and pasta

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined fried chicken and spaghetti fast food combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest parent for the primary protein component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken, fried) is used as the closest parent. No composite fast food combo meal code exists in FoodEx2 for this specific Jollibee product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Fried chicken) is the closest available term. No SNOMED CT concept exists for the full combo meal including spaghetti.

serving.description: 1 combo meal tray (2 pieces fried chicken + 1 regular Jolly Spaghetti)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Jollibee combo meal consisting of two pieces of bone-in Chickenjoy fried chicken (approximately 300 g) and one regular serving of Jolly Spaghetti (approximately 220 g), totalling roughly 520 g.

nutrient_source.title: Jollibee Philippines Nutrition Information — Chickenjoy (2 pcs) and Jolly Spaghetti (Regular); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken (NDB 05062) and canned tomato meat pasta sauce with pasta
nutrient_source.organization: Jollibee Foods Corporation Nutrition Data / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Jollibee Philippines publishes per-serving nutrition data for individual menu items. The combo meal values are derived by summing the published per-serving data for 2 pcs Chickenjoy (thigh + drumstick, ~300 g) and 1 regular Jolly Spaghetti (~220 g), then converting to per-100 g values for the combined 520 g meal. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fried chicken entries were used to cross-validate macros. This is an adapted composite estimate; exact Singapore Jollibee formulation may differ marginally.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — pasta and mixed fast food meal analogues; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee 2pc Chickenjoy with Jolly Spaghetti combo meal. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) white wheat spaghetti with tomato meat sauce GI ~45–50 per University of Sydney GI Database; (2) fried chicken breading (wheat flour) contributing additional rapidly digestible starch; (3) weighted composite estimate across the meal's carbohydrate sources (spaghetti ~60% of carb load, breading ~40%). Composite GI calculated as: (0.60 × 48) + (0.40 × 65) ≈ 55. GL per serving = (55 × 111.8 g carb per 520 g serving) / 100 ≈ 24.3. Analogous estimate; peer-reviewed GI tables used as evidence base.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~430 mg per 100 g; ~2,236 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 97% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg, primarily from the seasoned fried chicken breading and the sweetened meat spaghetti sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~24.3 per serving) and refined carbohydrate content from white pasta and wheat flour breading
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its high glycemic load of approximately 24.3 per serving, driven by refined white spaghetti and wheat-flour-breaded fried chicken, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion reduction and pairing with non-starchy vegetables is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and high sodium from deep-frying and processed meat sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This combo meal is high in saturated fat (~2.6 g/100 g), contains trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g from deep-frying), and delivers substantial dietary cholesterol (~52 mg/100 g) and sodium, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk; regular consumption is not recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee 2-Piece Chickenjoy with Jolly Spaghetti Meal (Combo D) is a popular fast food combo meal from Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore (including Bugis Village). The meal consists of two pieces of bone-in Chickenjoy fried chicken — typically a thigh and a drumstick — coated in Jollibee's signature crispy, well-seasoned wheat-flour breading and deep-fried until golden, served alongside a regular portion of Jolly Spaghetti, which is Jollibee's distinctively Filipino-style sweet tomato-based spaghetti topped with a meat sauce containing sliced hotdog pieces and ground meat. The fried chicken is juicy and flavourful with a crunchy exterior, while the spaghetti is sweeter than Italian-style pasta, reflecting Filipino taste preferences. The combined meal weighs approximately 520 g and delivers around 1,134 kcal per serving, making it a calorie-dense, high-sodium, high-carbohydrate meal. Nutritionally, it provides substantial protein (~66 g per serving) from the chicken, significant refined carbohydrates (~112 g per serving) from the pasta and breading, and notable saturated fat and sodium. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is tagged Halal-certified at Jollibee Singapore. Clinically, it is a high-concern food for hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load ~24.3), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol). It is best consumed occasionally and not as a regular dietary staple for individuals managing cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee 2pc Chickenjoy Spaghetti Meal?
Is Jollibee Chickenjoy Halal in Singapore?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information (per-serving data for individual items summed and converted to per-100 g) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fried chicken entries. The Singapore Jollibee formulation may differ marginally from the Philippines reference. GI is an analogous composite estimate based on peer-reviewed GI tables for white pasta and fried chicken breading components. No direct laboratory analysis of this specific Singapore combo meal is available.
notes: Per-100 g values are derived by summing Jollibee Philippines published per-serving nutrition data for 2 pcs Chickenjoy (~300 g) and 1 regular Jolly Spaghetti (~220 g), totalling 520 g, then dividing by 5.2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee published nutrition panel. GI is an analogous composite estimate (55, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white pasta with meat sauce and wheat-flour fried chicken breading. Halal status confirmed per Jollibee Singapore marketing and cuisine tags in source data. This is a fast food combo meal from a Filipino QSR chain; it is not a traditional Singaporean dish, so multilingual names are provided as descriptive translations rather than culturally established local names.

q1_food_identity.notes: canonical English name present and multilingual identity satisfied

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: core macros and nutrient source integrity pass

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from Jollibee Philippines published nutrition data and USDA cross-validation. Added all multilingual names for Singapore context. Computed per-100g values from composite meal weight of 520g.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55. Corrected character inconsistency in flat aliases for traditional Chinese name. Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified ontology match_type values from allowed set only.

Iteration 3: Resolved q8 failure — aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases. Performed character-level consistency audit of every string in aliases_by_language against flat aliases. Removed mixed-script Hindi alias '2 पीस சிக்கன்जॉय और जॉली स्पेगेटी मील' from flat aliases and replaced with the correct pure Devanagari form '2 पीस चिकनजॉय और जॉली स्पेगेटी मील' matching aliases_by_language exactly. Added romanized entry '2 Ketul Ayam Goreng dengan Spageti Jolly' to flat aliases to match aliases_by_language romanized key. All 18 flat alias entries now match verbatim to their aliases_by_language counterparts.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
